metrics.narrative: $700M annual spend ($450M direct, $350M indirect) with manual, paper-based procurement and contract management creating risk of contract leakage, non-compliance, and blocking WorkSafe's 5-year strategic transformation to lifecycle sourcing, contract management, and category management by FY26-27 (Matthew Mazzotta and team, 20 Jun 2025 intro call). Karen Bingham stated the driver is 'removing a lot of manual style activities' and 'automation' to support procurement/finance strategic objectives (1 Dec 2025 call). Mat Steenvoorden noted WorkSafe is 'exploring a new, fit-for-purpose digital solution to enhance automation in sourcing, evaluation, and contract management, moving away from manual processing' (9 Jul 2025 demo). No ROI or savings quantified yet (pre-commercials stage), but pain is operationally and strategically quantified: $700M spend under manual control, FY27 deadline to shift to lifecycle model, and Oracle Procurement 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required' (Next Step 12 Jul 2025).

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Karen Bingham (Senior Project Manager) and Rachel Leng (Senior Category Advisor - ICT) are the active buyer contacts executing the EOI/RFT process, but both are neutral project managers running a 'fair, arm's-length government procurement' with no preferential treatment for Zycus (Karen declined pre-EOI demo offer and stated demos are 'optional for all respondents' on 1 Dec 2025 call). They have no decision authority and are not advocates. Matthew Mazzotta (CPO, Executive Sponsor, EB) attended the 20 Jun 2025 intro call where Merlin Intake was an 'eye opener' (Next Step summary), but he has NOT engaged since—did not attend 9 Jul 2025 demo or 1 Dec 2025 EOI planning call (over 12 months silent as of 6 Jul 2026). Mat Steenvoorden (Decision Maker) attended intro and demo but has shown no advocacy or internal selling. Simon Nelis, Cameron Peel, Erin Westcott (evaluators) are similarly neutral. No WorkSafe stakeholder is promoting Zycus between procurement gates, de-risking the multi-stage governance approvals, or coaching Luke Dougherty on how to win. Risk: another supplier (or Oracle Cloud Procurement upgrade alternative) could out-lobby us at governance or in the RFT response, and we would have no internal early warning or counter-move. Gap is severe for a $250k, 12+ month government deal with stage-gated funding.

competition.narrative: Sole-source competitive position: no rival S2C vendor named across all three Avoma calls (20 Jun 2025, 9 Jul 2025, 1 Dec 2025) or in Next Step updates. Oracle Procurement Cloud is the INCUMBENT being REPLACED, not a competitor—WorkSafe is 'done with Oracle procurement software' and seeking 'something more AI driven, more automated, something a bit more easier to use' (Luke Dougherty's question and Karen's confirmation, 1 Dec 2025 call); Next Step 12 Jul 2025 states Oracle 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required'. Karen confirmed on 1 Dec call that the EOI will be 'released to nominated or selected suppliers' (plural), so other vendors will respond, but none are named or known. Luke asked Karen if she wanted a pre-EOI demo and she declined, treating all suppliers equally (1 Dec 2025). WorkSafe is NOT running a competitive bake-off with named peers (no 'Coupa vs SAP Ariba vs Zycus' shortlist); they are qualifying which S2C platforms can meet their criteria (Oracle integration, AI, S2C scope, automation). Our wedge: AI differentiation—Merlin Intake (Teams-embedded conversational procurement) was an 'eye opener' for Matthew Mazzotta (Next Step Jun 2025), and the 9 Jul demo showcased agentic negotiation agent, Contract Craft Pro AI clause recommendations, and Supplier Pulse Pro risk radar, all of which align with WorkSafe's explicit EOI requirement for 'AI capabilities and roadmap' (Karen 1 Dec 2025). Victorian government reference base (Dept Transport, Justice, Environment, Treasury & Finance already using Zycus per Luke 1 Dec 2025) de-risks WorkSafe's decision. Risk: we do not know who else is responding to the EOI/RFT, and with no champion we have no intel or early warning if a rival gains traction.

identify_pain.narrative: Manual, paper-based procurement and contract management across $700M annual spend is blocking WorkSafe's strategic transformation and creating operational/compliance risk. Specific pains articulated by buyer: (1) Oracle Procurement 'lacks the intelligence and productivity required' (Next Step 12 Jul 2025, Karen 1 Dec 2025), forcing reliance on manual sourcing, evaluation, contract drafting, and supplier onboarding (Mat Steenvoorden 9 Jul 2025 demo); (2) 'manual style activities' consuming procurement team capacity that should be strategic (Karen 1 Dec 2025); (3) 'risk of contract leakage and non-compliance' from paper-based workflows and no process controls for non-procurement users (9 Jul 2025 demo notes); (4) 'limited visibility and control... especially when it comes to contract obligations' and 'poor contract and supplier performance management' (intro call pain summary 20 Jun 2025); (5) 'unnecessary red tape in sourcing process' that needs removal while automating workflows (Next Step, 20 Jun 2025 call). Pain owner: Matthew Mazzotta (CPO) owns the strategic transformation ('transitioning from a sourcing focus to a lifecycle sourcing, contract management, and category management focus' per 5-year plan, 20 Jun 2025 call); Karen Bingham (Senior Project Manager) owns the project to solve it; procurement/finance teams are the daily sufferers of manual work. Compelling event: FY26-27 timeline to roll out lifecycle S2C solution integrated with the relevant stakeholder per WorkSafe's 5-year strategic plan (20 Jun 2025 call). No 'do-nothing' stated, but implication is WorkSafe remains manual and misses strategic plan milestone.

MSA/SOW structure not yet disclosed (pre-award). Luke Dougherty noted Zycus has already completed compliance and security reviews with multiple Victorian government departments (Transport, Justice, Environment, Treasury & Finance) so WorkSafe can inherit those clearances (1 Dec 2025: 'we're already working... we've already gone through all the different stages of compliance and security... so it's just gonna be an easy process to get that checkbox ticked'). No InfoSec, legal redline, or procurement channel details yet (too early-stage). Implementation scoping: Karen estimated ~6 months (1 Dec 2025 call), and cited a parallel WorkSafe project with similar timeline as reference. No partner/SI involvement mentioned. Oracle ERP integration is mandatory and will be a contracting/SOW line-item (Karen: 'bidirectional integration... that's imperative'). Gap: no visibility into MSA template, legal review process, signature authority, or DGS eService mechanics until post-award.

decision_process.narrative: Multi-stage Victorian government procurement mapped by Karen Bingham (1 Dec 2025 call): (1) EOI released 27 Jan 2026 to selected suppliers via Buying for Victoria tender site, 4-week response window, Q&A period first two weeks, Zycus submitted 1 Jun 2026; (2) EOI evaluation by end-April 2026 (Karen's governance target, met); (3) RFT (Request for Tender) release July 2026 (imminent per Next Step 6 Jul, Rachel confirmed update this week per 2 Jul email); (4) optional demos for shortlisted suppliers; (5) RFQ or RFP stage (~2 months per Karen); (6) governance approval to proceed with selected vendor; (7) contract award; (8) ~6-month implementation targeting FY27 go-live to meet WorkSafe's 5-year strategic plan. Karen emphasized funding is stage-gated: 'we've got the remit at the moment to complete an EOI process. We do have to take back the next step approach per the findings of the EOI back to our governance forums, and then that would be seeking permission to obviously if the team's happy to move to the next stage'—each phase (EOI → RFT → RFP → implementation) requires separate governance and budget approval. Decision authority: Matthew Mazzotta (CPO, business owner) sponsors governance submissions; Karen Bingham (Senior Project Manager) executes procurement; Mat Steenvoorden (DM) + Simon Nelis, Cameron Peel, Erin Westcott (evaluators) assess proposals; technical architecture team reviews AI capabilities. Rachel Leng stated DGS (Department of Government Services) eService requirement will apply at contracting stage but is not an EOI barrier. Process is on schedule: EOI eval complete, RFT due July 2026, decision end-2026 per original timeline.

sources: Avoma call 1 Dec 2025: Karen Bingham, Rachel Leng
Avoma demo 9 Jul 2025: criteria validated in demo flow
Avoma intro call 20 Jun 2025: pain points = contract/supplier mgmt priorities
decision_criteria.narrative: WorkSafe's EOI and upcoming RFT criteria are explicitly documented from the 1 Dec 2025 call with Karen Bingham and Rachel Leng: (1) bidirectional integration with the relevant stakeholder is 'imperative' and mandatory (Karen: 'That's that's imperative. That's very important'); (2) AI capabilities and roadmap visibility ('generalistic question around what are the AI capabilities or the road map for the companies'); (3) source-to-contract functional scope (sourcing, contract lifecycle, supplier management); (4) automation to remove manual procurement tasks; (5) contract management and supplier performance management (top priority per Next Step and 20 Jun 2025 intro call pain points); (6) compliance and process controls for non-procurement users (9 Jul 2025 demo discussion). The 9 Jul 2025 demo validated fit against these: Merlin Intake for end-user compliance, iContract for lifecycle management with AI clause recommendations, supplier risk radar, and Oracle integration architecture. Karen confirmed 'technical architecture teams will review capabilities' and demos are optional for shortlisted suppliers. No formal scoring rubric disclosed (government procurement confidentiality), but the six criteria above are confirmed as evaluation pillars. Zycus's AI differentiation (Merlin Intake, agentic negotiation, risk radar) directly addresses criteria (2) and (4); Oracle integration addresses (1); S2C suite addresses (3)(5)(6).

items.detail: Bidirectional Oracle ERP integration is 'imperative' and mandatory (Karen 1 Dec 2025) but not yet scoped, architected, or proven for WorkSafe's Oracle Cloud instance. Any integration complexity, cost overrun, or compatibility issue is disqualifying. RFT response must prove Oracle integration with a named Victorian gov reference (Dept Transport, Justice, Environment) running the same stack live, or this becomes the single highest technical risk to shortlist.

Matthew Mazzotta (CPO, Executive Sponsor, EB) has been silent for 12+ months (last engaged 20 Jun 2025 intro call) and holds governance approval authority for every stage (RFT → RFP → award → implementation funding per Karen 1 Dec 2025). His Executive Sponsor role is structural, not relational—no active buy-in, no relationship, no validation of the business case. Risk: he does not champion Zycus at governance, or another supplier gains his ear and flips his sponsorship.

No champion: no WorkSafe stakeholder is advocating for Zycus internally, de-risking rival positioning, or coaching Luke on how to win the multi-stage governance approvals. Karen/Rachel are neutral; Matthew (EB) is silent; Mat (DM) and evaluators show no advocacy. With unknown RFT competitors (July 2026, imminent) and stage-gated funding requiring governance sign-off at every phase, another supplier could out-lobby us and we would have no early warning or counter-move. This is the single highest loss risk.

Funding is stage-gated: only EOI-phase budget approved; RFT/RFP and implementation phases each require separate governance approval and budget allocation (Karen 1 Dec 2025: 'we've got the remit at the moment to complete an EOI process... take back the next step approach per the findings of the EOI back to our governance forums... seeking permission to move to the next stage'). Even a shortlist or RFT win does not guarantee funding to proceed. Any governance hesitation or budget constraint can stall the deal at any phase.

Unknown RFT competitors: Karen confirmed EOI will be 'released to nominated or selected suppliers' (plural, 1 Dec 2025), but no rival vendors are named. RFT due July 2026 (imminent per Next Step 6 Jul). With no champion and an arm's-length government process, Zycus has no intel on who else is responding or their positioning—risk of being out-positioned on price, AI roadmap, or Oracle integration proof, with no ability to counter.

Rachel Leng (Senior Category Advisor - ICT) and Karen Bingham (Senior Project Manager) are now the active buyer contacts (1 Dec 2025 call, 2 Jul email); CPO Matthew Mazzotta (EB, Executive Sponsor) has not engaged since 20 Jun 2025 intro call—economic buyer is passive, not bought-in.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and differentiated. WorkSafe's EOI explicitly requires 'AI capabilities and roadmap' (Karen 1 Dec 2025), and Merlin Intake was an 'eye opener' for CPO Matthew Mazzotta (Next Step Jun 2025 summary). The 9 Jul 2025 demo showcased Merlin Intake (Teams-embedded conversational procurement), agentic negotiation agent, Contract Craft Pro AI clause recommendations, and Supplier Pulse Pro risk radar—all align with WorkSafe's 'progressive appetite for AI' with 'safe and secure' implementation per organizational AI policy (20 Jun 2025 call). AI is the wedge vs Oracle Procurement incumbent ('lacks intelligence and productivity') and unknown RFT rivals. No evidence of under-positioning; AI story landed well in intro/demo and is a confirmed EOI evaluation criterion.

items.quote: Next Step and intro call 20 Jun 2025 identify 'contract management and supplier performance management is the top priority... poor contract and supply performance management... limited visibility and control especially when it comes to contract obligations.' 9 Jul demo showcased iContract end-to-end lifecycle mgmt, AI clause recommendations (Contract Craft Pro), supplier risk radar (Merlin Radar, Supplier Pulse Pro), proactive risk alerts, and third-party agency integration—exact fit to top priority.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on five of six confirmed evaluation criteria; Oracle integration (mandatory, imperative) is unvalidated and carries execution risk. AI capabilities (Merlin Intake, agentic negotiation, risk radar) directly meet EOI AI requirement and differentiate vs Oracle incumbent. S2C functional scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI per Products field) aligns with WorkSafe's lifecycle transformation plan. Automation and compliance process controls showcased in 9 Jul demo. Contract/supplier management priorities validated. Oracle bidirectional integration is 'imperative' (Karen 1 Dec 2025) but not yet scoped or proven—this is the single highest technical risk to shortlist.
